mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals broad economic expansion, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings expectations and thus positive forward returns for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its cycle lows is a robust leading indicator of recession. This triggers a flight to safety and a broad-based sell-off in equities as investors price in a sharp decline in corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses a similar mechanism as a recession indicator.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 produces positive returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains anchored, real interest rates fall. This signals a central bank falling 'behind the curve', increasing uncertainty and risk premia, which leads to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: A regression model shows the interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS levels is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, unexpectedly strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) is interpreted negatively by markets. It signals persistent demand-pull inflation, forcing the central bank to hike rates more aggressively, thereby increasing discount rates and lowering equity valuations.
failure_condition: The beta of S&P 500 20-day returns to positive RSAFS surprises is positive during periods where the FEDFUNDS rate has increased over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a slowdown in the goods-producing sector of the economy. This directly impacts the earnings of a large portion of the S&P 500, leading to negative forward returns as analysts downgrade forecasts.
failure_condition: The median 20-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) is a powerful leading indicator of broad economic activity, reflecting strong consumer balance sheets and credit availability. This boosts expectations for future GDP growth, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 6-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the Federal Funds rate (FEDFUNDS) tightens financial conditions and increases the discount rate applied to corporate earnings. This causes an immediate, negative repricing of equities over a very short horizon.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return in the week following a FEDFUNDS rate increase announcement is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) and acceleration in inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a stagflationary environment. This combination is toxic for equities, as it compresses corporate profit margins and increases the equity risk premium.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ % change < 1.0 AND CPIAUCSL YoY % change > 3.0
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has a positive median 60-day return during quarters that meet the trigger condition.
